fact: February 12 is exactly 43 days into the year - that's like counting to 43 Mississippi's every single day since New Year's!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when maple trees start getting ready to make syrup by moving sugar water up their trunks like tiny tree elevators!
We're still getting about 2 fewer hours of daylight than in summer around this time in northern climates, making everyone feel drowsy!
February 12 falls in the middle of Aquarius season - the water bearer constellation that looks like someone dumping a giant cosmic bucket!
In Antarctica, it's the middle of summer and penguins are experiencing 24-hour daylight!
This date sits exactly 37 days before the spring equinox - close enough that earthworms in warmer places start wiggling toward the surface!
February 12 is when many hibernating animals like bears are typically at their skinniest, having burned through half their winter fat stores while snoozing!
In Japan, plum trees called 'ume' often start blooming around this time, creating pink and white flower explosions even when snow is still on the ground!
When there's a full moon on February 12, it sits in the constellation Leo the Lion - roaring across the night sky!
In northern regions, this is prime time for seeing the aurora borealis because the long, dark nights give those dancing green lights the perfect stage!
February 12 marks the time when daylight in the Northern Hemisphere is increasing by about 2-3 minutes per day - like someone is slowly turning up the brightness on Earth!
In the Southern Hemisphere, cicadas are typically buzzing at maximum volume right now, creating nature's loudest summer soundtrack that can reach 120 decibels!
